topic: Rate of wax combustion vs wick diameter (presentation errors)
image_prompt: SVG: bar chart with deliberate presentation errors (axes swapped, bars instead of scatter). y-axis Wick diameter / mm (0 to 3.0, ticks every 0.5, minor every 0.1), x-axis categorical Rate of wax combustion / g min⁻¹ with labels 0.0651, 0.0743, 0.0850, 0.0921, 0.105. Five teal bars at heights 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5 mm.

SVG: multi-series smooth line graph, x-axis Year (1900 to 2020, ticks every 10, minor every 5), y-axis Billion cubic metres per year (0 to 3500, ticks every 500, minor every 100). Six regional series, smooth curves, colours teal/orange/magenta/green/violet/cyan (no red): Asia (highest, ~3150 at 2020, dramatic rise after 1960), North America (second highest in 2020, ~1450), Europe (~500 at 1950, ~880 at 2020), Africa (~630), South America (~430), Australia and Oceania (lowest, ~182).
